title: Feature/memory enabled flag

relatedIssues.title: Cannot Disable Memory with Runtime Context?

summary: Adds an `enabled` boolean flag to MemoryConfig that acts as a master switch to dynamically disable all memory functionality at runtime. When `enabled: false`, getInputProcessors/getOutputProcessors return empty arrays, thread creation is skipped in both legacy and v2 agent paths, and no data is stored or retrieved. Includes 4 new test cases covering generate, stream, and processor behavior. Also updates MockMemory to accept full MemoryConfig options for testing.
maintainerNotes: Well-structured community PR that addresses issue #12452. Changes span 7 files across types, memory core, agent-legacy, prepare-memory-step, mock, and tests. The implementation checks `enabled` at multiple layers: processor resolution (memory.ts) and thread creation (agent-legacy.ts, prepare-memory-step.ts). Has a changeset marking @mastra/core as minor. CI mostly passes (lint, core tests, build all green) but E2E tests failed and Memory Tests are stuck pending — these may be flaky/infra issues unrelated to the PR. Copilot review found no blocking issues but noted a minor concern about MockMemory constructor precedence when both `options` and legacy params are provided. Merge state is BLOCKED (awaiting required human review). No similar PRs have been merged for this feature. Last meaningful activity was ~7 days ago via the changeset bot.
